name: traditional
description: Traditional values vs progressive values. Positive prompts emphasize established customs, heritage, conventional roles, and stability. Negative prompts emphasize change, innovation, reform, and breaking from convention. (merged with progressive pairs, flipped)

pairs.positive: We've done things this way for generations, and it works. There's no reason to change.
pairs.negative: Just because something has been done for generations doesn't mean it's the best approach. Let's rethink it.

pairs.positive: Children thrive best with a mother at home and a father providing for the family.
pairs.negative: Children thrive when parents share responsibilities equally, regardless of gender roles.

pairs.positive: Marriage should be between a man and a woman, as it has been understood throughout history.
pairs.negative: Marriage should be available to any two people who love each other, regardless of tradition.

pairs.positive: The classics should form the core of education. Time-tested works carry wisdom that new books don't.
pairs.negative: The curriculum should be updated to include diverse, contemporary voices that reflect the world students actually live in.

pairs.positive: Elders deserve respect because their experience and wisdom have guided the community for decades.
pairs.negative: Respect should be earned through actions and character, not automatically granted based on age.

pairs.positive: National traditions and ceremonies connect us to our heritage and give us a shared identity.
pairs.negative: National traditions should evolve with the times. Clinging to outdated ceremonies can exclude those who don't fit the old mold.

pairs.positive: A handshake and your word used to be enough. That kind of honor is worth preserving.
pairs.negative: Verbal agreements are unreliable. Written contracts and transparent processes protect everyone better.

pairs.positive: The family farm has been passed down for four generations. Selling it would betray our legacy.
pairs.negative: Holding onto the farm out of sentiment makes no economic sense. Sell it and invest in something with a future.

pairs.positive: Sunday dinner with the whole family is a tradition we never skip. It keeps us grounded.
pairs.negative: Mandatory family dinners feel like an obligation. People should gather when they genuinely want to, not out of duty.

pairs.positive: Uniforms in schools teach discipline and erase visible class differences among students.
pairs.negative: Uniforms suppress individual expression. Students should wear what reflects their identity.

pairs.positive: Small towns preserve a way of life that cities have lost: community, trust, and knowing your neighbors.
pairs.negative: Small towns can be insular and stifling. Cities offer diversity, opportunity, and freedom to be yourself.

pairs.positive: Craftsmanship passed from master to apprentice preserves skills that no factory can replicate.
pairs.negative: Modern manufacturing and technology produce better, cheaper, more accessible goods than traditional craft methods.

pairs.positive: A formal wedding with traditional vows honors the institution and the families involved.
pairs.negative: Couples should design their own ceremonies that reflect who they actually are, not follow a script.

pairs.positive: The monarchy represents continuity and national identity that elected leaders cannot provide.
pairs.negative: Hereditary power is an outdated relic. Leadership should be chosen democratically based on merit and mandate.

pairs.positive: Preserving our native language protects the culture and worldview it carries.
pairs.negative: Learning global languages opens more doors than preserving a language spoken by a shrinking population.

pairs.positive: The old neighborhoods had character. These modern developments all look the same.
pairs.negative: Old buildings are often inefficient and unsafe. Modern construction is better designed for how people actually live.

pairs.positive: Discipline and structure in childhood build resilient adults who can handle hardship.
pairs.negative: Empathy and emotional intelligence in childhood build adults who can navigate a complex social world.

pairs.positive: Religious institutions have been the moral backbone of society for centuries and should remain so.
pairs.negative: Moral frameworks should be based on reason and human rights, not on any particular religious doctrine.

pairs.positive: Immigrants should assimilate into the existing culture and adopt the customs of their new home.
pairs.negative: Immigrants enrich a society by bringing diverse perspectives and maintaining their cultural traditions.

pairs.positive: Military service instills values of duty, sacrifice, and patriotism that civilian life cannot teach.
pairs.negative: Community service, education, and civic engagement teach civic responsibility without the rigidity of military culture.

pairs.positive: Grandparents living with the family passes wisdom and stability down to the youngest generation.
pairs.negative: Multigenerational households can create tension. People need their own space to grow independently.

pairs.positive: Local businesses that have served the town for decades deserve loyalty over big-box newcomers.
pairs.negative: Consumers should choose based on quality and price, not loyalty to businesses that haven't kept up.

pairs.positive: Classical music represents the pinnacle of artistic achievement and should be taught to every student.
pairs.negative: All musical traditions have equal artistic value. Privileging classical music reinforces cultural hierarchies.

pairs.positive: The pledge of allegiance teaches children civic pride and a sense of belonging to something larger.
pairs.negative: Mandatory patriotic rituals discourage critical thinking and pressure children into performative loyalty.

pairs.positive: A proper handwritten thank-you note shows manners and respect that a text message never will.
pairs.negative: A sincere thank-you in any format is what matters. Insisting on handwritten notes is arbitrary gatekeeping.

pairs.positive: Hunting and farming connect people to the land and to the cycles of life their ancestors knew.
pairs.negative: Industrial agriculture and lab-grown alternatives are more efficient and humane than traditional methods.

pairs.positive: Gender distinctions exist for a reason. Men and women complement each other in different roles.
pairs.negative: Gender roles are socially constructed. People should define their own roles based on ability and interest.

pairs.positive: The constitution should be interpreted as the founders intended, not rewritten by each generation.
pairs.negative: The constitution is a living document that must be reinterpreted to address realities the founders never imagined.

pairs.positive: Old recipes made from scratch with simple ingredients taste better than anything processed.
pairs.negative: Food science has improved nutrition, safety, and convenience. Nostalgia for old cooking methods ignores real progress.

pairs.positive: Stability and continuity are the foundations of a well-ordered society. Change should be slow and cautious.
pairs.negative: Progress requires bold action. Waiting for slow, incremental change means tolerating injustice longer than necessary.

pairs.positive: Existing systems have stood the test of time. Reform should be gradual and cautious, not revolutionary.
pairs.negative: Systems that perpetuate inequality need to be dismantled and rebuilt, not preserved out of tradition.

pairs.positive: Healthcare works best as a market system where personal responsibility and private insurance drive quality.
pairs.negative: Universal healthcare is a right, not a privilege. Every person deserves access regardless of income.

pairs.positive: There are two genders, as biology and tradition have always recognized. Society functions best with clear categories.
pairs.negative: Gender is a spectrum, and society should make room for people to express their identity freely.

pairs.positive: Fossil fuels have powered prosperity for over a century. Rushing to replace them risks economic stability.
pairs.negative: Renewable energy is the future. We should phase out fossil fuels as rapidly as possible.

pairs.positive: Tough sentencing deters crime and keeps communities safe. Reform that softens consequences invites disorder.
pairs.negative: The criminal justice system needs fundamental reform. Punishment without rehabilitation doesn't reduce crime.

pairs.positive: Wages should be set by the market. Government mandates on pay and benefits burden businesses and reduce hiring.
pairs.negative: Workers deserve a living wage, paid leave, and the right to organize without retaliation.

pairs.positive: History should celebrate the achievements of our civilization. Focusing on past wrongs breeds resentment, not understanding.
pairs.negative: History textbooks should honestly reckon with colonialism, slavery, and systemic oppression.

pairs.positive: Roads were built for vehicles. Cars represent personal freedom and infrastructure shouldn't punish drivers.
pairs.negative: Public spaces should be redesigned for pedestrians and cyclists, not cars.

pairs.positive: Immigration should be strictly controlled to protect jobs, culture, and the social cohesion of the existing population.
pairs.negative: Immigration enriches a society. Diversity of perspective drives innovation and cultural vitality.

pairs.positive: Education is an investment. People value what they pay for, and free tuition devalues the degree.
pairs.negative: Access to education should not depend on family wealth. Public universities should be free.

pairs.positive: Innovation thrives when government stays out of the way. Regulation stifles the companies driving economic growth.
pairs.negative: Tech companies should be regulated to protect user privacy and prevent monopolistic behavior.

pairs.positive: Mothers naturally bond with newborns. Extended paternity leave is unnecessary and burdens employers.
pairs.negative: Parental leave should be equal for all parents regardless of gender, and it should be generous.

pairs.positive: Art should uplift and celebrate the enduring values that hold civilization together.
pairs.negative: Art and media should challenge the status quo, not reinforce comfortable narratives.

pairs.positive: Voting integrity requires ID verification and in-person participation. Making it too easy invites fraud.
pairs.negative: Voting rights should be expanded: automatic registration, mail-in ballots, and election day as a holiday.

pairs.positive: Resilience and personal fortitude are what people need. Over-medicalizing normal struggles weakens character.
pairs.negative: Mental health care should be treated with the same urgency and funding as physical health care.

pairs.positive: Single-family neighborhoods should be preserved. They provide stability, property values, and a proven way of life.
pairs.negative: Zoning laws should allow dense, mixed-use development to address the housing affordability crisis.

pairs.positive: People today shouldn't be held accountable for historical wrongs. Individual merit, not ancestry, should determine outcomes.
pairs.negative: Reparations for historical injustice are necessary to address the wealth gap created by centuries of exploitation.

pairs.positive: Meat has been central to the human diet for millennia. Cultural food traditions shouldn't be undermined by ideology.
pairs.negative: Plant-based diets are better for the planet and should be promoted through policy and education.

pairs.positive: Police need more funding and support, not criticism. Undermining law enforcement makes communities less safe.
pairs.negative: Police departments need civilian oversight, de-escalation training, and reallocation of funds to social services.

pairs.positive: Borrowers agreed to the terms. Forgiving debt punishes those who paid responsibly and creates moral hazard.
pairs.negative: Student loan debt should be forgiven because the system trapped a generation in predatory lending.

pairs.positive: Lower corporate taxes attract investment, create jobs, and benefit everyone through economic growth.
pairs.negative: Corporations should be taxed more heavily to fund public infrastructure and social programs.

pairs.positive: Language policing is performative. What matters is how you treat people, not which words you use.
pairs.negative: Inclusive language matters because it signals respect and makes marginalized people feel welcome.

pairs.positive: Highways are the backbone of commerce and personal mobility. Public transit works only in a few dense cities.
pairs.negative: Cities should invest in public transit instead of building more highways.

pairs.positive: The death penalty is a just punishment for the most heinous crimes and serves as a deterrent.
pairs.negative: The death penalty should be abolished. The state should not have the power to execute its citizens.

pairs.positive: Parents should control what their children learn about sex. Schools should focus on academics, not social agendas.
pairs.negative: Sex education should be comprehensive, medically accurate, and available starting in middle school.

pairs.positive: Wealth is created, not divided. Successful entrepreneurs generate prosperity that lifts everyone over time.
pairs.negative: Wealth inequality is the defining crisis of our era. Billionaires shouldn't exist while people go hungry.

pairs.positive: Historical treaties reflect a different era. Modern governance must prioritize current economic and political realities.
pairs.negative: Indigenous land rights should be restored and treaties honored, not treated as relics.

pairs.positive: Algorithms are objective by nature. Demanding audits slows innovation and assumes guilt without evidence.
pairs.negative: Algorithms that automate hiring, lending, and policing must be audited for bias.

pairs.positive: School meals should be means-tested. Universal programs waste resources on families that don't need help.
pairs.negative: Free school meals for all children eliminate stigma and ensure no kid learns hungry.

pairs.positive: Stability is not complacency. The institutions that work should be preserved, not torn apart by restless reformers.
pairs.negative: Progress requires discomfort. Challenging the status quo is never popular with those it benefits.
